# M7 Integration Gate Report

**Story:** M7.5 — M7 Integration Gate (init → CLAUDE.md + .claude/rules/ + delimiter round-trip)
**Generated by:** @dev (Dex — Builder persona) acting as gate test author + verdict
**Date:** 2026-04-25
**Verdict:** **PASS**
**Reviewer disposition:** PASS the M7 milestone for v1.5.0 closeout. All three PRD § M7 gate criteria are objectively verified by `npm test`. No CRITICAL findings; one informational note (the Yotzer skill-hash drift inherited from M6.7 §6.1) carries forward unchanged — it is owned by M8.x and is orthogonal to M7's surface area.

This is a machine document. Audience: `@po` (sign-off), `@pm` (M8 closeout / v1.5.0 GA tag decision), `@devops` (release tag trigger), `@qa` (gate verdict cross-check).

---

| 2 | `.claude/rules/` populated with six seed files (FR-050) — each non-empty | **PASS** | `tests/m7/integration/test-m7-gate.js` Gate 2 |
| 3 | Update simulation: expert edit inside `KAIZEN:EXPERT:*` preserved byte-for-byte; `KAIZEN:FRAMEWORK:*` updated to new canonical (CON-007) | **PASS** | `tests/m7/integration/test-m7-gate.js` Gate 3 (re-uses M7.4 fixtures + helpers) |

**Overall verdict:** **PASS** — All 3 PRD § M7 gate criteria pass the integration suite. M8 closeout and v1.5.0 GA tag are unblocked from M7's perspective.

| AC | Description | Status | Evidence |

| **Gate 2 (FR-050)** | `.claude/rules/` exists with exactly six named files matching the M7.1 seed list | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 2 (six entries match `boundary.md`, `cells.md`, `yotzer.md`, `doctor.md`, `language-policy.md`, `commit-conventions.md`) |
| **Gate 2 (FR-050)** | Each rule file ≥10 lines (no stub bodies) | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 2 (all six are 79–112 lines per L2 source) |
| **Gate 2 (FR-050)** | Each rule body matches the L2 template byte-for-byte (single source of truth) | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 2 (Buffer.compare of written vs `.kaizen-dvir/instructions/templates/rules/{name}.md`) |
| **Gate 3 (CON-007)** | EXPERT block bytes preserved (sha256) across `kaizen update` | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 3 (sha256 equality + verbatim string equality) |
| **Gate 3 (CON-007)** | FRAMEWORK block replaced with new canonical content | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 3 (canonical marker `Framework block updated to v1.6.0` present in post-update bytes) |
| **Gate 3 (CON-007)** | Four delimiters intact, in canonical order, after update | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Gate 3 (count == 1 each; FW:START < FW:END < EX:START < EX:END) |
| **Vocabulary (FR-051, D-v1.5-08)** | Vocabulário section disambiguates Célula and Agente | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Vocabulary (Célula + tiers/grupo orquestrado/unidade de distribuição; Agente + persona/componente interno) |
| **Language Policy (D-v1.4-06)** | Every user-facing artifact (CLAUDE.md + 6 rules) is pt-BR | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Language (zero EN-dominant paragraphs across 7 files) |
| **Language Policy (D-v1.4-06)** | `kaizen init` stdout/stderr is pt-BR | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Language CLI guard (canonical pt-BR strings present; heuristic clean) |
| **Heuristic Sensitivity (story Dev Notes)** | Heuristic flags deliberately English content; does not flag deliberately pt-BR content | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Language self-check (positive + negative control) |
| **Determinism (Commandment IV, story DoD)** | No time-of-day / random / env-leak; reproducible across environments | **PASS** | test-m7-gate.js Determinism (two parallel sandboxes produce byte-identical CLAUDE.md + rules/*) |
| **CI / `npm test` (Commandment IV)** | All tests run under `npm test` with no manual steps | **PASS** | `tests/run.js` discovers `tests/m7/integration/test-m7-gate.js` automatically |

| **CON-002** | CommonJS, ES2022 | **PASS** | New files declare `'use strict';`, use `require`, no ESM imports |

The 2 cross-channel hash failures called out in the M6.7 gate report (§6.1) no longer reproduce in the current `main` snapshot — they pass when the test sandbox path is stable. The drift is still latent (the comment at `_personaRelPath()` lines 683-690 of `cell-registry.js` still writes an absolute path) but does not currently fail any test. Recommend M8 closeout track this as a hardening item.

## 4. Constitutional Compliance Check

| Commandment | Compliance | Evidence |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **I — CLI First** | PASS | M7's full surface (`kaizen init`, `kaizen update`) is CLI. The gate suite invokes the CLI binaries directly via `spawnSync` and via the `runUpdate` programmatic entry. No UI dependency. |
| **II — Authority Boundaries** | PASS | M7.5 stays in `@dev` test-author scope per the story `executor: @dev` declaration. The verdict role here is the gate-test verdict (not @qa milestone sign-off — that remains the story's `quality_gate: @qa` field, deferred to the next reviewer). No `git push` (delegated to `@devops`). No production code modified. |
| **III — No Invention** | PASS | Every gate AC traces to PRD v1.5 § M7 Gate (criteria 1–3), FR-049, FR-050, FR-051, AC-027, CON-007, D-v1.4-06, D-v1.5-08. Performance / line-count thresholds match the PRD verbatim. |
| **IV — Quality First** | PASS | Quality gate executed (this report). Verdict PASS issued. All test files lint + typecheck clean. CI ready under `npm test`. |
| **V — Documentação Contínua** | PASS | Story M7.5 Dev Agent Record updated; this `gate-report.md` committed; change logs append-only. |
| **VI — Human-in-Loop** | PASS | Verdict surfaces in the report for `@po` review. PASS verdict is auto-actionable; no waiver needed. |
| **VII — Evolução Incremental** | PASS | M7.5 reuses the M7.4 helpers (`extractExpertBlockBytes`, `hashString`, `readFixture`, `setupDelimiterSandbox`, `runUpdate`, `rmRoot`) verbatim — REUSE > ADAPT > CREATE. The new `_helpers-gate.js` only adds the heuristic + sandbox-init helpers genuinely missing from M7.1–M7.4. Zero duplication of M7.4 round-trip logic. |

### Language Policy Audit (D-v1.4-06)

| Surface | Audit Result |
|---------|-------------|
| `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (generated by init) | **PASS** — zero EN-dominant paragraphs |
| `.claude/rules/boundary.md` | **PASS** |
| `.claude/rules/cells.md` | **PASS** |
| `.claude/rules/yotzer.md` | **PASS** |
| `.claude/rules/doctor.md` | **PASS** |
| `.claude/rules/language-policy.md` | **PASS** |
| `.claude/rules/commit-conventions.md` | **PASS** |
| `kaizen init` stdout (canonical run) | **PASS** — pt-BR completion line + next-steps block; heuristic clean |
| `kaizen init` stderr (canonical run) | **PASS** — empty on success |
| Heuristic positive control (English prose) | **FLAGGED as expected** |
| Heuristic negative control (pt-BR prose) | **NOT FLAGGED as expected** |

The heuristic has been calibrated conservatively (EN ≥ 3 stop-words AND EN > 2× pt-BR + 1 buffer) so paragraphs that mix pt-BR prose with a few EN command names ("kaizen init", "boundary.md") do not flip a flag. Sensitivity proven by the deliberate-English self-check; specificity proven by the deliberate-pt-BR self-check.

---

## 5. Determinism Audit

The story DoD calls out: "no `Date.now()` in content assertions, no random data, no network calls — reproducible in any environment." All five conditions verified:

| Determinism Vector | Verification |
|--------------------|--------------|
| No time-of-day in artifacts | Two parallel `kaizen init` runs in independent sandboxes produce byte-identical CLAUDE.md + every rule file (test: `M7.5 / Determinism`) |
| No random data | All test inputs are fixtures committed under `tests/m7/fixtures/`; no `Math.random()`, `crypto.randomBytes()`, `crypto.randomUUID()` in the gate test paths |
| No network calls | All tests stay on the local FS; `runInit` spawns the local `bin/kaizen.js`; `runUpdate` calls the M7.4 in-process helper |
| No env leakage | The runtime helpers save/restore `process.env.KAIZEN_PROJECT_ROOT` and `process.cwd()` (per M7.4 `_helpers-delimiter.js` § runUpdate) |
| CI ready | `tests/run.js` already auto-discovers `tests/m7/integration/test-*.js`; `npm test` reports 995/0 |

---

## 6. Known Issues

### 6.1 Yotzer skill-hash drift (CARRIED FROM M6.7 §6.1 — informational only)

**Status:** Observed but NOT failing in the current snapshot. The non-determinism root cause (`_personaRelPath()` writing absolute paths into skill bodies) still exists in `cell-registry.js`, but the cross-channel test sandboxes apparently use stable enough paths now to not trip the assertion.

**Severity:** INFO (no current failure to remediate from M7.5's perspective).

**Why this does not affect M7.5:**
- M7's surface is `kaizen init` writing CLAUDE.md + `.claude/rules/`, which are deterministic (proven by §5).
- The skill-hash drift lives in `.claude/commands/Kaizen/Yotzer*.md`, which M7 does not touch.
- The M7 gate criteria do not include skill-body content stability.

**Remediation owner:** M8.x (skill registration code path), per M6.7 §6.1.

### 6.2 No new known issues

The M7.5 gate found no new issues. All 10 gate tests pass, all 995 repo tests pass, lint + typecheck pass.

---

## 7. Decision

**Gate verdict:** **PASS**

**Rationale:** All three PRD § M7 Gate de Passagem criteria pass at the integration level:

1. **Gate 1 (init writes CLAUDE.md with FR-049 sections + delimiters):** Verified by 3 tests in `test-m7-gate.js`. Line count is 197 (within 150–250 budget). All 10 FR-049 sections present in canonical order. Four CON-007 delimiters present exactly once each, in canonical order, with non-empty FRAMEWORK and minimal EXPERT (only the invitation HTML comment).

2. **Gate 2 (`.claude/rules/` populated with six seeds):** Verified by 1 test in `test-m7-gate.js`. Directory exists; exactly six files with the FR-050-canonical names; each ≥ 10 lines; each byte-for-byte equal to its L2 source — proving the M7.3 wiring transports M7.1 author content with zero drift.

3. **Gate 3 (update round-trip preserves EXPERT, replaces FRAMEWORK):** Verified by 1 test in `test-m7-gate.js`. Sha256 of the EXPERT block is byte-identical pre/post `kaizen update`. The new canonical FRAMEWORK marker appears verbatim. Four delimiters remain intact in canonical order. This re-runs the M7.4 fixtures through the M6 update engine — the canonical M7 ↔ M6 contract surface.

Additional gates (Vocabulary, Language Policy on files + CLI, Heuristic Sensitivity, Determinism) all PASS — exceeding the minimum gate criteria.

**Recommended actions:**

1. **@po (Pax) — sign-off requested:** approve M7 milestone with PASS verdict. `approved_by: <po>` recorded in story M7.5.
2. **@pm (Morgan) — M8 closeout decision:** M7 ↔ M8 dependency satisfied. M8.7 (parallel) and v1.5.0 GA tag are unblocked from M7's perspective.
3. **@devops (Gage) — proceed to v1.5.0 GA tag** when M8.7 PASS lands.
4. **No remediation stories required from M7.5.** §6.1 (Yotzer skill-hash drift) remains an M8.x backlog item; it does not block M7 closeout.

## 10. Notes for Downstream

### For M8.7 (parallel — Wave 8):
- M7 owns `tests/m7/`; M8.7 owns `tests/m8/integration/`. No file-level overlap.
- The M6.7 `expected-package-contents.txt` was NOT touched by M7.5 (M7.5 added no files to the publish-shape envelope; the new `tests/m7/integration/` content is test-only and excluded from the npm pack `files` whitelist). M8.7 may modify that file freely without conflict.

### For v1.5 GA tag:
- M7 is signed off PASS at the integration level.
- The Yotzer skill-hash drift (M6.7 §6.1) is the remaining informational item; it does NOT block GA from M7's perspective.

### For v1.6+ planning:
- The 10-section structure of CLAUDE.md (FR-049) is the v1.5 contract; adding/removing sections in v1.6+ requires a decision record (per Epic KZ-M7 § Compatibility Requirements).
- The four delimiter markers are a durable contract; breaking their syntax requires a framework MAJOR version bump.
- The six seed rule filenames are a stable additive set; new rules in v1.6+ are append-only.
